questions.question: Define polymorphism and explain its importance in OOP.
keyPoints: Polymorphism means 'many forms' - same interface different implementations
Enables code reusability and flexibility
Allows treating different object types uniformly through common interface
Supports dynamic method dispatch at runtime

questions.question: Differentiate between method overloading and method overriding with examples.
keyPoints: Overloading: same class, same name, different parameters (compile-time)
Overriding: different classes (parent-child), same signature (runtime)
Python simulates overloading with default arguments
Overriding uses inheritance and polymorphic behavior

questions.question: What is Duck Typing in Python? How does it relate to polymorphism?
keyPoints: Duck typing: object's suitability determined by methods/attributes, not type
Based on phrase: 'If it walks like a duck and quacks like a duck...'
Python uses duck typing extensively for polymorphism
No explicit type checking needed - just need required methods

questions.question: Explain Abstract Base Classes (ABC) in Python with an example.
keyPoints: ABC cannot be instantiated directly
Uses abc module: from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
Abstract methods have no implementation, must be overridden by subclasses
Ensures subclasses provide specific implementations

questions.question: What are dunder methods? Give two examples related to operator overloading.
keyPoints: Dunder methods: double underscore methods like __method__
Also called magic or special methods
__add__ for + operator, __sub__ for - operator
__eq__ for ==, __lt__ for <, __len__ for len()

questions.question: How does isinstance() function support polymorphic behavior?
keyPoints: isinstance(obj, Class) returns True if obj is instance of Class
Enables runtime type checking
Can check against multiple classes using tuple
Used in polymorphic functions to handle different types differently

questions.question: Create a Python program demonstrating polymorphism using an abstract base class Shape with subclasses Circle and Rectangle having a common method area().
keyPoints: Define ABC Shape with abstract method area()
Circle subclass: implements area using radius (πr²)
Rectangle subclass: implements area using length and breadth
Create list of Shape objects and call area() polymorphically
Demonstrate runtime polymorphism - correct method called based on actual object type

questions.question: Explain operator overloading in Python. Write a class to overload + and - operators for adding/subtracting complex numbers.
keyPoints: Operator overloading uses dunder methods
Define __add__(self, other) for + and __sub__(self, other) for -
Complex number has real and imaginary parts
Method returns new Complex object with calculated values
Demonstrate usage: c1 + c2, c1 - c2

questions.question: Write a Python program demonstrating Duck Typing for a polymorphic 'draw' function that works with different shape objects.
keyPoints: Define classes Circle, Square, Triangle without common parent
Each class has its own draw() method
Function draw_shapes(objects) calls object.draw() regardless of type
No inheritance needed - duck typing in action
Demonstrates Python's philosophy: 'Easier to ask forgiveness than permission'

questions.question: Explain Method Resolution Order (MRO) with an example involving multiple inheritance. How can you view MRO?
keyPoints: MRO defines order Python searches for methods in class hierarchy
Use ClassName.mro() or ClassName.__mro__ to view
C3 linearization algorithm used in Python
For Diamond inheritance: D inherits B and C which inherit A
MRO ensures parent visited only once, preserves order
